title: Wonderware historian base URL hardcoded to plaintext http:// — Basic-auth credentials sent in clear
detail: `this.baseUrl = \`http://${this.serverHost}:${this.serverPort}/Historian/v1\`;` is hardcoded to http. The constructor builds a Basic-auth header from username/password/domain (lines 95-102: `this.authHeader = \`Basic ${credentials}\``) and `fetchWithAuth` (line 318-320) sends it on every request. With no TLS option, historian credentials (potentially Windows domain creds) traverse the network base64-encoded but unencrypted. On a millos.net (https) page this is also a mixed-content request the browser will block, so the feature silently fails AND leaks creds where it does work. There is no `protocol`/`useSsl`/`https` config field in WonderwareConnectionConfig to override it.
fix: Add an optional `useSsl?: boolean` (or `baseUrlOverride`) to WonderwareConnectionConfig in HistorianInterface.ts and derive scheme (`https://` default) instead of hardcoding `http://`. Defaulting to https is the safe posture for SCADA credentials.
_area: L4 API/Errors · SCADA adapter contracts + bridge/historian

title: REST batch read does not validate response shape before .map — malformed 200 response trips false connection-loss
detail: `const data: RESTBatchResponse = await response.json(); const tagValues = data.tags.map(...)`. There is no check that `data` is an object or that `data.tags` is an array. A 200 response with an unexpected/empty body (`{}`, an array, a server returning `{error:...}`) throws `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'map')`. Because `readTags` is called from `readAllTags` → `poll()` (line 290) whose catch routes to `handleConnectionError()` (line 293), a single malformed-but-OK response is misclassified as a lost connection, incrementing reconnectAttempts and ultimately calling disconnect() which drops all subscribers (see separate finding).
fix: After json(), guard: `if (!data || !Array.isArray(data.tags)) throw new Error('Malformed batch response');` and validate each entry has tagId/value/quality/timestamp (mirror isValidBatchTag in messageValidation.ts) before parseTagResponse. Same guard belongs on readTag's `data` at line 145.
_area: L4 API/Errors · SCADA adapter contracts + bridge/historian

title: After 5 reconnect failures REST adapter silently disconnects and clears all subscribers with no consumer notification
detail: In handleConnectionError: `if (this.reconnectAttempts >= 5) { this.disconnect(); }`. disconnect() (lines 108-123) clears `this.subscribers` and `this.globalSubscribers`. Consumers that registered callbacks via subscribe() are never told the stream died — their callbacks just stop firing forever, and there is no error event/callback surfaced. `lastError` stays the generic 'Connection lost'. UI bound to live tags will freeze on stale data with no indication. Compare WebSocketAdapter which keeps retrying up to 10 times rather than tearing down subscribers.
fix: On terminal failure, set a distinct lastError (e.g. 'Reconnect limit reached') and emit a connection-status change rather than silently clearing subscribers; or do not clear subscribers in the auto-disconnect path so a later manual reconnect can resume. Needs a product call on desired reconnect ceiling/UX.
_area: L4 API/Errors · SCADA adapter contracts + bridge/historian

title: PI adapter maps response.Items without verifying it is an array (response-shape gap → silent empty result)
detail: Every PI read does `const data: PIStreamValuesResponse = await response.json(); return this.mapPIValuesToHistoryPoints(data.Items);` (lines 151-152, 179-180, 205-206; getLatestValue line 219 maps `[data]` assuming the body is a single PIValue). If PI returns a body without `Items` (error envelope, partial response), `items.map` in mapPIValuesToHistoryPoints (line 363) throws. HistorianRouter.fetchFromRemote catches it (line 273) and returns `[]`, so a malformed response degrades to 'no data' with only a logger.warn — charts show empty trends and the operator gets no signal that the historian returned garbage.
fix: Guard each: `const items = Array.isArray(data?.Items) ? data.Items : []; return this.mapPIValuesToHistoryPoints(items);`. For getLatestValue, validate `data` is an object with a Timestamp before mapping.
_area: L4 API/Errors · SCADA adapter contracts + bridge/historian

title: Wonderware adapter maps response.Data without verifying it is an array (response-shape gap)
detail: `const data: WWHistoryResponse = await response.json(); return this.mapWWValuesToHistoryPoints(data.Data);` (lines 166-167, 191-192, 217-218; getLatestValue line 228 maps `[data]`). No check that `data.Data` exists/is an array. A 200 response missing `Data` throws in mapWWValuesToHistoryPoints (line 332 `items.map`). Caught upstream and converted to empty result, hiding the contract violation from the user.
fix: Guard: `const rows = Array.isArray(data?.Data) ? data.Data : []; return this.mapWWValuesToHistoryPoints(rows);` and validate the single-value body in getLatestValue before mapping.
_area: L4 API/Errors · SCADA adapter contracts + bridge/historian

title: No time-range validation before historian queries (start>end, NaN dates, intervals=0 reach adapters)
detail: getRecordedValues/getInterpolatedValues/getPlotValues (lines 126-166) and the underlying PI/WW adapters perform zero validation that `startTime <= endTime`, that dates are valid (an Invalid Date yields `.toISOString()` throw), or that `intervals`/`intervalMs` are positive. A reversed range silently produces an empty or server-error result; an Invalid Date throws inside the adapter URL build. There is no single choke point enforcing the contract documented in HistorianInterface.ts.
fix: Add a guard at the top of getBlendedData / each public method: if either date is invalid (`Number.isNaN(startTime.getTime())`) or `endTime < startTime`, return [] (or throw a typed RangeError) before delegating. Cheap, prevents adapter-level surprises.
_area: L4 API/Errors · SCADA adapter contracts + bridge/historian

title: getPlotValues fallback divides duration by intervals with no zero/negative guard (Infinity / negative interval)
detail: On TrendData failure the fallback computes `const intervalMs = Math.floor(durationMs / intervals); return this.getInterpolatedValues(tagId, startTime, endTime, intervalMs);` (lines 212-214). If a caller passes `intervals: 0` (reachable via HistorianQueryOptions.intervals), this is `durationMs / 0 = Infinity` → `resolutionMS=Infinity` in the InterpolatedData URL. If startTime>endTime, durationMs is negative → negative resolutionMS. Either produces a malformed historian request.
fix: `const safeIntervals = intervals > 0 ? intervals : 100; const durationMs = Math.max(0, endTime.getTime() - startTime.getTime()); const intervalMs = Math.max(1, Math.floor(durationMs / safeIntervals));`
_area: L4 API/Errors · SCADA adapter contracts + bridge/historian

title: msToIsoDuration emits PT0S for sub-second intervals and malformed durations for negative/NaN input
detail: `const seconds = Math.floor(ms / 1000); if (seconds < 60) return \`PT${seconds}S\`;` — any intervalMs < 1000 (e.g. a 500ms interpolation request) floors to `PT0S`, which PI Web API rejects as an invalid interval. A negative intervalMs yields `PT-5S` and NaN yields `PTNaNS`. getInterpolatedValues passes this straight into the URL (line 166-171) with no lower bound.
fix: Clamp at the top: `const safeMs = Number.isFinite(ms) && ms > 0 ? ms : 1000;` and ensure the smallest unit is at least 1 (`const seconds = Math.max(1, Math.floor(safeMs/1000))`).
_area: L4 API/Errors · SCADA adapter contracts + bridge/historian

title: Timed-out WebSocket connect still schedules background reconnects after the connect promise rejects
detail: On connect timeout (line 95-98) the code `reject(new Error('Connection timeout')); this.ws?.close();`. Closing the socket fires `onclose` → `handleDisconnect()` (line 134-135), and because `isDisconnecting` is still false, handleDisconnect schedules an exponential-backoff reconnect (lines 383-393). So a caller whose `connect()` rejected (and who may construct a fresh adapter and retry) now has an orphaned background reconnect loop on the rejected socket, potentially doubling connection attempts and never surfacing as connected/disconnected to the caller.
fix: Set `this.isDisconnecting = true` (or a dedicated `connectAborted` flag) before calling `this.ws?.close()` in the timeout handler so onclose does not start a reconnect for a connect that the caller already saw fail.
_area: L4 API/Errors · SCADA adapter contracts + bridge/historian

title: WS batch/snapshot handler trusts t.tagId/value/quality/timestamp without per-field guards despite validator gaps
detail: In handleMessage, batch/snapshot does `msg.tags.map((t) => ({ tagId: t.tagId, value: t.value, quality: this.parseQuality(t.quality), timestamp: t.timestamp }))` (lines 321-327). isValidWSMessage→isValidBatchTag does validate these fields, so today it is safe, but parseQuality (line 356) calls `quality.toUpperCase()` — if a future message path bypasses the validator or quality is the empty string the result is fine, but if quality were undefined (e.g. validator relaxed) this throws. The 'update' path is safer (parseTagValue uses `?? 'GOOD'`). Inconsistency: update defends with nullish coalescing, batch relies entirely on the external validator. Low-risk today, brittle to validator drift.
fix: Make parseQuality null-safe (`const q = String(quality ?? '').toUpperCase();`) and apply `?? Date.now()` / `?? 0` defaults in the batch map to match parseTagValue, so the two ingest paths fail identically.
_area: L4 API/Errors · SCADA adapter contracts + bridge/historian

title: PI getWebId assumes /points?path= returns a single PIPointResponse object; PI returns Items[] for path queries
detail: `const data: PIPointResponse = await response.json(); this.webIdCache.set(tagId, data.WebId); return data.WebId;` (lines 329-331). PI Web API point lookups by path commonly return either a single point or, for some endpoints, an envelope. If the body is an error/empty object, `data.WebId` is undefined and gets cached (line 330) — the undefined WebId is then cached permanently, so every subsequent read for that tag short-circuits via the cache check `if (cachedWebId !== undefined)` at line 313... note: caching `undefined` actually does NOT poison the cache (it's not set when undefined-keyed), but caching a literal undefined value via set() stores `undefined` and the `!== undefined` guard then treats a cache-miss-stored-undefined as a hit returning undefined. Net: a one-time bad lookup is cached as a permanent failure for that tag until disconnect.
fix: Validate before caching: `if (!data || typeof data.WebId !== 'string') { return null; }` then `this.webIdCache.set(tagId, data.WebId); return data.WebId;` so transient failures are not cached as permanent.
_area: L4 API/Errors · SCADA adapter contracts + bridge/historian

title: poll() swallows all errors as 'connection lost' — distinguishes neither HTTP status nor parse failures
detail: `private async poll() { ... try { ... } catch { this.handleConnectionError(); } }` (lines 286-295). Any throw — auth 401/403, malformed JSON, a single bad batch — is treated identically as a transient connection error driving exponential backoff. A 401 (bad API key) will never recover via reconnect and just burns 5 attempts then tears down subscribers. No status-code-specific handling.
fix: In readTags/readTag surface the HTTP status on the thrown error, and in poll's catch distinguish auth/4xx (terminal, set lastError and stop) from 5xx/network (retry).
_area: L4 API/Errors · SCADA adapter contracts + bridge/historian

title: OPC quality mask comment says 16-bit but math assumes 8-bit; values >0xFF mis-classified
detail: mapOpcQuality does `const major = (opcQuality >> 6) & 0x3;` while the doc comment (lines 56-58) states 'OPC quality is 16-bit: high 8 bits = major'. For a standard 8-bit OPC DA quality byte, `(q>>6)&0x3` correctly extracts the 2-bit major field, so current behavior is right for 8-bit codes. But if Wonderware's REST API returns the full 16-bit OPC quality word (which some AVEVA endpoints do), the major bits are at >>14, and `(q>>6)&0x3` reads substatus bits instead — every value would be mis-graded. Contract ambiguity between comment and code.
fix: Confirm the WW REST API's Quality field width. If 8-bit, fix the comment to say 8-bit. If 16-bit is possible, normalize first: `const q8 = opcQuality > 0xff ? (opcQuality >> 8) : opcQuality; const major = (q8 >> 6) & 0x3;`
_area: L4 API/Errors · SCADA adapter contracts + bridge/historian

title: REST getStatistics readsPerSecond/uptime computed from connectTime even across reconnect churn; no avgReadLatency reset
detail: getStatistics (lines 270-280) computes `uptime = (Date.now() - this.connectTime)/1000` and divides cumulative readCount by it. On reconnect, connect() resets connectTime (line 91) but stats (readCount/totalLatency) are never reset, so readsPerSecond is computed against a fresh uptime denominator with a stale cumulative numerator, yielding inflated rates right after reconnect. Diagnostic-only, but misleading for operators reading the stats panel.
fix: Either reset the stats object in connect()/disconnect(), or track a separate `statsWindowStart` independent of connectTime so rate denominators match the counter accumulation window.
_area: L4 API/Errors · SCADA adapter contracts + bridge/historian

title: applyCorrelations divides by engHigh-engLow with no guard — NaN/Infinity if a correlated tag has zero range
detail: `const normalizedCorrelated = ((correlatedValue.value as number) - correlatedDef.engLow) / (correlatedDef.engHigh - correlatedDef.engLow);` (lines 497-499). If a correlated tag definition has engHigh === engLow (degenerate/misconfigured range), this is division by zero → Infinity/NaN, which then propagates into the simulated value via correlationEffect (line 502-505) and ultimately into TagValue.value. Although tick() later clamps to engLow/engHigh of the *target* tag (SimulationAdapter line 411), a NaN survives Math.max/Math.min as NaN and can reach the 3D bridge.
fix: Guard the denominator: `const range = correlatedDef.engHigh - correlatedDef.engLow; if (range <= 0) return;` (skip that correlation) before computing normalizedCorrelated.
_area: L4 API/Errors · SCADA adapter contracts + bridge/historian
